questions.question: If sin(θ) = 3/5 and θ is in the second quadrant, find the exact value of tan(θ).
questions.explanation: In quadrant II, sin(θ) = 3/5 is positive. Use sin²(θ) + cos²(θ) = 1: (3/5)² + cos²(θ) = 1, so cos²(θ) = 16/25. Since θ is in quadrant II, cos(θ) = -4/5. Then tan(θ) = sin(θ)/cos(θ) = (3/5)/(-4/5) = -3/4.

questions.question: A ladder leans against a wall, forming a 65° angle with the ground. If the ladder is 10 meters long, how far up the wall does it reach? Round to the nearest tenth.
questions.explanation: The height is the opposite side to the 65° angle. Use sin(65°) = height/10, so height = 10·sin(65°) ≈ 10(0.906) ≈ 9.1 meters.

questions.question: Simplify: (sin²(x) - cos²(x))/(sin(x) - cos(x)). Do not use a calculator.
questions.explanation: Factor the numerator as a difference of squares: sin²(x) - cos²(x) = (sin(x) - cos(x))(sin(x) + cos(x)). Cancel the common factor (sin(x) - cos(x)) from numerator and denominator to get sin(x) + cos(x).

questions.question: If cos(θ) = -1/3 and π < θ < 3π/2, find sin(θ).
questions.explanation: θ is in quadrant III where sine is negative. Use sin²(θ) + cos²(θ) = 1: sin²(θ) = 1 - 1/9 = 8/9, so sin(θ) = ±2√2/3. Since θ is in quadrant III, sin(θ) = -2√2/3.

questions.question: Verify the identity: (1 - sin(x))/(cos(x)) = cos(x)/(1 + sin(x)). Show one key step.
questions.answer: Cross-multiply to get (1 - sin(x))(1 + sin(x)) = cos²(x); then 1 - sin²(x) = cos²(x).
questions.explanation: Cross-multiplying gives (1 - sin(x))(1 + sin(x)) = cos²(x). Expand the left side as 1 - sin²(x). By the Pythagorean identity, 1 - sin²(x) = cos²(x), confirming equality.

questions.question: A point on the unit circle is located at angle 7π/6 radians from the positive x-axis. What are its coordinates?
questions.explanation: 7π/6 is in quadrant III (π + π/6). The reference angle is π/6. Cosine and sine are both negative in quadrant III: cos(7π/6) = -√3/2 and sin(7π/6) = -1/2.
